title: diplopappus rigidus
intro: Diplopappus rigidus, also known as the stiff-head or rigid diplopappus, is a type of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Diplopappus rigidus include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. Diplopappus rigidus is typically prepared for herbal use through infusion, decoction, or tincture, and can be consumed as a tea or added to topical creams for its medicinal benefits.
